path_variant_matrix.contract: Guest absolute paths under / are rewritten into the materialized rootfs unless they are explicit host pseudo-filesystems or bind targets.
Relative dirfd paths remain relative to the tracee context after the traced dirfd/cwd is validated as an allowed rootfs or bind host path.

Relative paths whose dirfd/cwd, parent traversal, or symlink resolution would escape the rootfs or declared bind hosts are completed with a deterministic errno instead of being silently passed through.
Paths that already point inside the rootfs are not double-prefixed.
/proc, /dev, and /sys stay on their controlled host/pseudo-filesystem routes; /proc/self/exe is explicitly emulated.
Absolute paths containing .. do not use the rootfs-fd fast path; they are rewritten through scratch storage or rejected as too long.
Declared bind paths take precedence over rootfs prefixing while preserving guest-visible path semantics.
linkat, renameat, and renameat2 rewrite old and new path operands independently.
AF_UNIX bind/connect paths are rewritten when safe, nscd probes are skipped, and overlong rewritten paths do not corrupt sockaddr_un.

Script shebangs, interpreter arguments, bracket argv, standard CMake BLAS option argv, and long linker argv lists with many separate flash_attn object arguments are preserved when routed through the bundled loader.
The internal rootfs fd cannot be closed by the tracee and remains available for rootfs-fd rewrites.

boundary_value_matrix.contract: Null pointers and zero-capacity reads fail closed; unterminated strings are capped and NUL-terminated.
PATH_MAX-1 paths remain representable and rootfs-prefixed overflow returns ENAMETOOLONG or leaves the original syscall path untouched with a diagnostic.
A too-small getcwd buffer returns ERANGE without truncating guest cwd state.
Short sockaddr lengths, non-AF_UNIX sockets, abstract/relative socket paths, and rewritten paths at or beyond sun_path capacity are handled deterministically.
EXEC_REWRITE_MAX_ARGC and EXEC_REWRITE_MAX_SCRATCH prevent stack corruption while preserving normal long linker commands with many separate object path arguments.
mmap/mremap/brk requests at, below, and above the configured guard threshold produce traceable allow/deny decisions.
setreuid/setregid/setresuid/setresgid preserve fields passed as -1 and update only requested container credential slots.
Normal exits return the raw exit code and signaled processes report 128+signal for direct runtime and PTY routes.

branch_decision_matrix.contract: Every should_rewrite_path return branch is represented by either a runnable path probe or an explicit pseudo-filesystem probe.
Bind precedence, no-op paths, successful rootfs prefixing, and overflow are tracked as separate test obligations.

The fd-relative fast path, validated relative pass-through, scratch fallback, and explicit unsafe-path errno completion are split so parent segments, binds, symlinks, and overflow cannot hide behind the same success case.

branches: success -> sockaddr path and length updated
branch_decision_matrix.contract: AF_UNIX rewriting is decomposed by sockaddr validity, socket namespace, nscd skip, length overflow, and success.
Exec routing is tested as a branch matrix because this path owns bracket argv, standard CMake option argv, many-argument linker argv, scripts, and loader recursion safety.
Large allocation diagnostics separate tracing from active ENOMEM denial.
Credential emulation covers reads, writes, sentinel preservation, and child inheritance.
Guest cwd reporting is split from host cwd recovery and short-buffer handling.
Tracee lifecycle coverage records exit status, signal status, child trace events, seccomp events, and stale pid pruning.

coverage.contract: Path xattr calls stay inside the materialized rootfs or a declared bind path, and libcow copy-up preserves optional xattrs before metadata mutation.
coverage.fast_check: Static verifier checks the direct rewrite switch and libcow xattr interceptors.

coverage.contract: Create, remove, and rename path arguments are rewritten from guest absolute paths to rootfs or bind host paths before the kernel sees them.
coverage.fast_check: Static verifier checks path-rewrite coverage entries.

coverage.contract: Link/symlink/readlink paths preserve guest-visible behavior. Hardlink closure is fail-closed: the current linkat copy fallback is only a content compatibility workaround and cannot satisfy linkat_hardlink_semantics until same st_dev/st_ino identity, st_nlink growth/decrement, and write-through behavior are proven without rootfs helper mutations.
coverage.fast_check: Static verifier checks linkat/symlinkat emulation, /proc/self/exe handling, and the non-promoting hardlink semantics plan.

coverage.contract: Open/stat/access/cwd syscalls resolve against guest rootfs state, report guest cwd, preserve ERANGE for short getcwd buffers, and protect the internal rootfs fd from tracee close.
coverage.fast_check: Static verifier checks getcwd emulation, ERANGE path, faccess emulation, rootfd close emulation, and rewrite table coverage.

coverage.contract: Exec paths resolve under rootfs or PATH, scripts route through their interpreter, argv is rebuilt for the bundled loader, and state tracks the guest executable path.
coverage.fast_check: Static verifier checks loader argv rebuild markers and rootfs exec path tracking.

coverage.contract: AF_UNIX bind/connect sockaddr paths that name guest absolute paths are rewritten to rootfs or bind host paths, with sockaddr length updated. This keeps code-server, Codex, and GPU bridge control sockets inside the container-visible rootfs instead of Android host /tmp.
coverage.fast_check: Static verifier checks rewrite_unix_sockaddr_arg plus bind/connect syscall table coverage.

coverage.contract: Container-root credential calls are answered from tracee state without changing Android app credentials, and pid reconciliation rejects reused host pids before restart.
coverage.fast_check: Static verifier checks credential emulation hooks and existing runtime-contract pid identity tests.

coverage.contract: Android-blocked startup/probe syscalls return deterministic Linux-compatible fallback errno values so glibc, libuv, and runtime probes continue through portable paths.
coverage.fast_check: Static verifier checks seccomp errno hooks and userland completion suppression.

coverage.contract: NUMA policy calls blocked by Android app seccomp return ENOSYS, matching kernels without NUMA support and allowing OpenBLAS/llama.cpp style callers to fall back.
coverage.fast_check: Static verifier checks the NUMA range and explicit home-node fallback.

coverage.contract: Build diagnostics can trace memory-growth syscalls, summarize large/failed allocation requests, and optionally return ENOMEM before Android LMK pressure kills pdockerd or the UI.
coverage.fast_check: Static verifier checks that memory syscalls are explicitly traced and covered by the direct syscall inventory.

non_syscall_contracts.contract: PTY sessions use forkpty plus execve, expose resize/read/write, and return either WEXITSTATUS or negative WTERMSIG; the direct tracer returns 128+signal for signaled root tracees.
Container start reconciles a verified live pid and rejects stale pid reuse using recorded process start time.
libcow covers open/openat/creat/truncate/fopen, chmod/chown/utime/xattr, fd metadata, close, and dup lifecycle copy-up behavior.

Docker attach/exec PTY sessions must preserve stdin/stdout attachment, resize propagation, Ctrl-C/SIGINT delivery to descendant process groups, and Docker-compatible exit or signal-coded status; current coverage is a planned device gate, not a closure claim.

Dockerfile RUN snapshotting must consume a conservative changed-path manifest for traced or statically-known filesystem mutations; current fast coverage locks down chmod wildcard path manifests while broader traced mutation manifests remain open.

heavy_cases.checks: Guest paths in logs remain under rootfs/binds; getcwd returns guest cwd and ERANGE for too-small buffers; open/stat/access errno values match Linux.

heavy_cases.command:
PDOCKER_PACKAGE="${PDOCKER_PACKAGE:-io.github.ryo100794.pdocker}"; ROOTFS="${ROOTFS:?set ROOTFS to the app-visible rootfs path}"; adb shell "run-as $PDOCKER_PACKAGE sh -lc 'cd files && PDOCKER_DIRECT_TRACE_SYSCALLS=1 pdocker-runtime/docker-bin/pdocker-direct run --mode exec --rootfs $ROOTFS --workdir / -- /bin/sh -lc "set -eu; p=/tmp/pdocker-direct-meta; rm -f \\$p; : > \\$p; chmod 600 \\$p; touch -m \\$p; chown 0:0 \\$p 2>/dev/null || true; python3 - <<PY
import os
p=\"/tmp/pdocker-direct-meta-py\"
open(p,\"w\").close()
os.chmod(p,0o640)
os.utime(p,(123456789,123456790))
try: os.setxattr(p,b\"user.pdocker\",b\"ok\")
except (AttributeError,OSError) as e: print(\"xattr_skip\", getattr(e,\"errno\",None))
else: assert os.getxattr(p,b\"user.pdocker\") == b\"ok\"
assert oct(os.stat(p).st_mode & 0o777) == \"0o640\"
print(\"metadata_ok\")
PY"'"
heavy_cases.checks: No Android host paths leak into trace logs; metadata changes are visible only through the guest path.

heavy_cases.command:
PDOCKER_PACKAGE="${PDOCKER_PACKAGE:-io.github.ryo100794.pdocker}"; ROOTFS="${ROOTFS:?set ROOTFS to the app-visible rootfs path}"; adb shell "run-as $PDOCKER_PACKAGE sh -lc 'cd files && PDOCKER_DIRECT_TRACE_SYSCALLS=1 pdocker-runtime/docker-bin/pdocker-direct run --mode exec --rootfs $ROOTFS --workdir / -- /bin/sh -lc "set -eu; d=/tmp/pdocker-direct-mut; rm -rf \\$d; mkdir -p \\$d/a; : > \\$d/a/file; mv \\$d/a/file \\$d/renamed; test -f \\$d/renamed; rm \\$d/renamed; rmdir \\$d/a; python3 - <<PY
import ctypes, errno, os
libc=ctypes.CDLL(None, use_errno=True)
AT_FDCWD=-100; SYS_openat2=437; SYS_name_to_handle_at=264
class OpenHow(ctypes.Structure):
    _fields_=[(\"flags\",ctypes.c_ulonglong),(\"mode\",ctypes.c_ulonglong),(\"resolve\",ctypes.c_ulonglong)]
how=OpenHow(0,0,0)
rc=libc.syscall(SYS_openat2, AT_FDCWD, b\"/tmp/pdocker-direct-mut\", ctypes.byref(how), ctypes.sizeof(how))
if rc >= 0: os.close(rc)
else: assert ctypes.get_errno() in (errno.ENOSYS, errno.EINVAL, errno.EOPNOTSUPP, errno.EACCES, errno.ENOENT)
rc=libc.syscall(SYS_name_to_handle_at, AT_FDCWD, b\"/tmp/pdocker-direct-mut\", 0, 0, 0)
assert rc == -1
assert ctypes.get_errno() in (errno.EFAULT, errno.ENOSYS, errno.EOPNOTSUPP, errno.EPERM, errno.ENOENT)
print(\"mutation_ok\")
PY"'"
heavy_cases.checks: Mutations land under the guest rootfs or declared bind target; unsupported probes return documented errno.

heavy_cases.command: PDOCKER_PACKAGE="${PDOCKER_PACKAGE:-io.github.ryo100794.pdocker}"; ROOTFS="${ROOTFS:?set ROOTFS to the app-visible rootfs path}"; adb shell "run-as $PDOCKER_PACKAGE sh -lc 'cd files && PDOCKER_DIRECT_TRACE_SYSCALLS=1 pdocker-runtime/docker-bin/pdocker-direct run --mode exec --rootfs $ROOTFS --workdir / -- /bin/sh -lc \"set -eu; exe=\\\$(readlink /proc/self/exe); test \\\"\\\$exe\\\" = /bin/sh; d=/tmp/pdocker-direct-links; rm -rf \\\$d; mkdir -p \\\$d; echo ok > \\\$d/src; ln \\\$d/src \\\$d/hard; ln -s src \\\$d/sym; test \\\"\\\$(cat \\\$d/hard)\\\" = ok; test \\\"\\\$(readlink \\\$d/sym)\\\" = src; echo proc_links_ok\"'"
heavy_cases.checks: /proc/self/exe reports the guest executable; link/symlink emulations preserve content-visible behavior. This case is not sufficient for linkat hardlink closure because it does not prove inode identity or link-count preservation.

heavy_cases.command: Planned non-promoting Android device gate: run pdocker-direct with a linkat/ln probe that creates /tmp/pdocker-direct-hardlink/src, links it to /tmp/pdocker-direct-hardlink/hard, records stat st_dev/st_ino/st_nlink for both paths before and after writing through one name, unlinks one name, then saves the JSON/log artifact plus restart recovery evidence for any hardlink/CoW metadata update.
heavy_cases.checks: Fail-closed expectations while the copy fallback remains: this planned artifact is non-promoting and must not close the contract unless src and hard report identical st_dev and st_ino, both report st_nlink >= 2 after linkat, a write through either name is visible through the other name, unlinking one name decrements st_nlink while the remaining path survives, invalid flags/escape paths return Linux-compatible errno, and restart recovery proves no partial hardlink index or divergent copy-fallback artifact is promoted.

heavy_cases.command:
PDOCKER_PACKAGE="${PDOCKER_PACKAGE:-io.github.ryo100794.pdocker}"; ROOTFS="${ROOTFS:?set ROOTFS to the app-visible rootfs path}"; adb shell "run-as $PDOCKER_PACKAGE sh -lc 'cd files && PDOCKER_DIRECT_TRACE_EXEC=1 pdocker-runtime/docker-bin/pdocker-direct run --mode exec --rootfs $ROOTFS --workdir / --env PATH=/tmp:/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin -- /bin/sh -lc "set -eu; cat > /tmp/pdocker-argv-probe <<SH
#!/bin/sh
echo script_ok
SH
chmod +x /tmp/pdocker-argv-probe; /bin/echo absolute_ok | grep absolute_ok; pdocker-argv-probe alpha beta | grep script_ok; /tmp/pdocker-argv-probe gamma delta | grep script_ok; long=; i=0; while [ \\$i -lt 320 ]; do long=\"\\$long flash_attn.comp.cpp.o\"; i=\\$((i+1)); done; test \\${#long} -gt 4096; /bin/sh -lc '\"'case \"\\$1\" in *flash_attn.comp.cpp.o*flash_attn.comp.cpp.o*) echo pdocker-long-argv-ok ;; *) echo bad-long-argv; exit 7 ;; esac'\"' sh \"\\$long\" | grep pdocker-long-argv-ok"'"
heavy_cases.checks: argv[0], interpreter arguments, bracket paths, and long linker-style argv strings over PATH_MAX match guest expectations; no rootfs binary is execve'd directly from app data.

heavy_cases.command: Create AF_UNIX server/client probes under guest absolute socket paths, then run them through pdocker-direct with rootfs and declared bind path coverage.
heavy_cases.checks: bind(AF_UNIX) creates the socket under the rewritten host path/rootfs path, connect(AF_UNIX) reaches it through the same mapping, and sockaddr lengths match the rewritten path. This remains planned until a device artifact proves bind/connect through the direct executor.

heavy_cases.command:
PDOCKER_PACKAGE="${PDOCKER_PACKAGE:-io.github.ryo100794.pdocker}"; ROOTFS="${ROOTFS:?set ROOTFS to the app-visible rootfs path}"; adb shell "run-as $PDOCKER_PACKAGE sh -lc 'cd files && PDOCKER_DIRECT_TRACE_SYSCALLS=1 pdocker-runtime/docker-bin/pdocker-direct run --mode exec --rootfs $ROOTFS --workdir / -- /bin/sh -lc "python3 - <<PY
import os, sys
assert os.getuid() == 0 and os.geteuid() == 0
os.setuid(123)
assert os.getuid() == 123 and os.geteuid() == 123
if hasattr(os, \"getresuid\"): assert os.getresuid() == (123,123,123)
pid=os.fork()
if pid == 0:
    assert os.getuid() == 123
    os._exit(0)
_, status = os.waitpid(pid, 0)
assert os.WIFEXITED(status) and os.WEXITSTATUS(status) == 0
print(\"pid_credentials_ok\")
PY"'"
heavy_cases.checks: Credential calls report container-root state and forked tracees inherit credential state; reused pid rejection remains covered by the fast runtime-contract verifier.

heavy_cases.command:
PDOCKER_PACKAGE="${PDOCKER_PACKAGE:-io.github.ryo100794.pdocker}"; ROOTFS="${ROOTFS:?set ROOTFS to the app-visible rootfs path}"; adb shell "run-as $PDOCKER_PACKAGE sh -lc 'cd files && PDOCKER_DIRECT_TRACE_SYSCALLS=1 pdocker-runtime/docker-bin/pdocker-direct run --mode exec --rootfs $ROOTFS --workdir / -- /bin/sh -lc "python3 - <<PY
import ctypes, errno
libc=ctypes.CDLL(None, use_errno=True)
def call(n,*args):
    ctypes.set_errno(0); rc=libc.syscall(n,*args); return rc, ctypes.get_errno()
for nr in (425,426,427,435,436):
    rc,err=call(nr,0,0,0,0,0,0)
    assert rc == -1 and err == errno.ENOSYS, (nr,rc,err)
for nr in (99,293):
    rc,err=call(nr,0,0,0,0,0,0)
    assert rc in (0,-1), (nr,rc,err)
print(\"startup_fallbacks_ok\")
PY"'"
heavy_cases.checks: Fallback syscalls return ENOSYS or the documented success emulation without SIGSYS leakage.

heavy_cases.command:
PDOCKER_PACKAGE="${PDOCKER_PACKAGE:-io.github.ryo100794.pdocker}"; ROOTFS="${ROOTFS:?set ROOTFS to the app-visible rootfs path}"; adb shell "run-as $PDOCKER_PACKAGE sh -lc 'cd files && PDOCKER_DIRECT_TRACE_SYSCALLS=1 pdocker-runtime/docker-bin/pdocker-direct run --mode exec --rootfs $ROOTFS --workdir / -- /bin/sh -lc "python3 - <<PY
import ctypes, errno
libc=ctypes.CDLL(None, use_errno=True)
for nr in (235,236,237,238,239,450):
    ctypes.set_errno(0)
    rc=libc.syscall(nr,0,0,0,0,0,0)
    assert rc == -1 and ctypes.get_errno() == errno.ENOSYS, (nr,rc,ctypes.get_errno())
print(\"numa_enosys_ok\")
PY"'"
heavy_cases.checks: Every NUMA policy syscall returns ENOSYS under direct runtime.

heavy_cases.command:
PDOCKER_PACKAGE="${PDOCKER_PACKAGE:-io.github.ryo100794.pdocker}"; ROOTFS="${ROOTFS:?set ROOTFS to the app-visible rootfs path}"; adb shell "run-as $PDOCKER_PACKAGE sh -lc 'cd files && PDOCKER_DIRECT_TRACE_MEMORY=1 PDOCKER_DIRECT_MEMORY_GUARD=1 PDOCKER_DIRECT_TRACE_MEMORY_THRESHOLD=1M PDOCKER_DIRECT_MEMORY_GUARD_MIN_AVAILABLE=64G pdocker-runtime/docker-bin/pdocker-direct run --mode exec --rootfs $ROOTFS --workdir / -- /bin/sh -lc "python3 - <<PY
import mmap, sys
try:
    mmap.mmap(-1, 2 * 1024 * 1024)
except OSError as e:
    print(\"memory_guard_errno\", e.errno)
    sys.exit(0)
raise SystemExit(3)
PY"'"
heavy_cases.checks: Guarded large mmap returns ENOMEM and the log includes pdocker-direct-memory deny/summary lines.

heavy_cases.command:
PDOCKER_PACKAGE="${PDOCKER_PACKAGE:-io.github.ryo100794.pdocker}"; ROOTFS="${ROOTFS:?set ROOTFS to the app-visible rootfs path}"; adb shell "run-as $PDOCKER_PACKAGE sh -lc 'cd files && PDOCKER_DIRECT_TRACE_SYSCALLS=1 PDOCKER_DIRECT_TRACE_PATHS=1 pdocker-runtime/docker-bin/pdocker-direct run --mode exec --rootfs $ROOTFS --workdir / -- /bin/sh -lc "set -eu; mkdir -p /tmp/pdocker-boundary/a /tmp/pdocker-boundary/b; : > /tmp/pdocker-boundary/a/file; stat /tmp/pdocker-boundary/./a/../a/file >/dev/null; stat /proc/self/exe >/dev/null; python3 - <<PY
import ctypes, errno, os, socket
libc=ctypes.CDLL(None, use_errno=True)
AT_FDCWD=-100
SYS_getcwd=17
buf=ctypes.create_string_buffer(1)
ctypes.set_errno(0)
rc=libc.syscall(SYS_getcwd, ctypes.addressof(buf), 1)
assert rc == -1 and ctypes.get_errno() == errno.ERANGE, (rc, ctypes.get_errno())
# Exercise short AF_UNIX paths and nscd skip path without requiring nscd to exist.
s=socket.socket(socket.AF_UNIX, socket.SOCK_STREAM)
try:
    s.connect('/var/run/nscd/socket')
except OSError as e:
    assert e.errno in (errno.ENOENT, errno.ECONNREFUSED, errno.ENAMETOOLONG)
finally:
    s.close()
try:
    os.unlink('/tmp/pdocker-boundary/a/escape')
except FileNotFoundError:
    pass
os.symlink('/data', '/tmp/pdocker-boundary/a/escape')
fd=os.open('/tmp/pdocker-boundary/a', os.O_RDONLY | getattr(os, 'O_DIRECTORY', 0))
try:
    try:
        os.stat('escape', dir_fd=fd)
    except OSError as e:
        assert e.errno == errno.EXDEV, e
    else:
        raise AssertionError('relative escape unexpectedly succeeded')
finally:
    os.close(fd)
print('boundary_matrix_ok')
PY"'"
heavy_cases.checks: Exercises parent-segment path handling, /proc/self/exe special handling, getcwd ERANGE, and AF_UNIX nscd skip behavior under the direct runtime.

heavy_cases.command: ROOTFS="${ROOTFS:?set ROOTFS to the container rootfs path}" scripts/container-direct-probe.sh --memory-guard
heavy_cases.checks: Generic container test route runs without adb or APK install, checks argv preservation including many separate flash_attn object names and standard CMake-like flags, verifies /usr/bin/[ bracket execution, checks /proc/self/exe guest path behavior, and exercises the large allocation guard.

heavy_cases.command: Run docker exec -it through the Engine API and the native PTY helper: verify stdin echo, resize/SIGWINCH, exit 7, and a SIGTERM-killed command.
heavy_cases.checks: PTY read/write/resize works; normal exits return raw code; signaled processes report the documented signal-coded result; this is a planned device gate until an adb script captures artifact evidence.

heavy_cases.command: make -C docker-proot-setup/src/overlay && bash docker-proot-setup/src/overlay/test_cow.sh
heavy_cases.checks: Hardlink copy-up protects lower files across writes, metadata, fd operations, and optional xattrs.

heavy_cases.command: Start a container with attach-before-start, then exercise docker attach plus docker exec -it over the Engine hijack path: send Enter, Ctrl-C/SIGINT, SIGTERM, window resize, and EOF while collecting Engine status/log artifacts.
heavy_cases.checks: Attach does not race start; stdin/stdout streams remain connected; Ctrl-C reaches the foreground descendant process group without killing pdockerd; resize triggers SIGWINCH; EOF drains logs; inspect/wait records Docker-compatible exit or 128+signal status.

heavy_cases.command:
PDOCKER_PACKAGE="${PDOCKER_PACKAGE:-io.github.ryo100794.pdocker}"; ROOTFS="${ROOTFS:?set ROOTFS to the app-visible rootfs path}"; adb shell "run-as $PDOCKER_PACKAGE sh -lc 'cd files && PDOCKER_DIRECT_TRACE_SYSCALLS=1 pdocker-runtime/docker-bin/pdocker-direct run --mode exec --rootfs $ROOTFS --workdir / -- /bin/sh -lc \"python3 - <<PY
import ctypes, errno, os
libc=ctypes.CDLL(None, use_errno=True)
AT_FDCWD=-100
def call(n,*a):
    ctypes.set_errno(0); rc=libc.syscall(n,*a); return rc, ctypes.get_errno()
# openat2/name_to_handle_at/linkat/readlinkat/faccessat2 parity probes stay explicit.
print(\"errno_parity_probe_planned\")
PY\"'"
heavy_cases.checks: Unsupported or denied syscalls return syscall-specific Linux errno values instead of permissive success; probes include startup fallbacks, path boundary denial, linkat invalid flags and escape errors, readlinkat short buffers, and openat2/name_to_handle_at behavior.

heavy_cases.command: Create one bind mount, one project volume, and one named volume, then run direct open/stat/rename/linkat plus AF_UNIX bind/connect probes under each mount path with PDOCKER_DIRECT_TRACE_PATHS=1.
heavy_cases.checks: Every filesystem syscall and AF_UNIX socket path resolves through the same mediation table; guest paths cannot escape via relative dirfd, symlink, parent segments, or mixed bind/rootfs dual-path operations. This remains planned until a device artifact captures the bind/project-volume/named-volume evidence.

heavy_cases.command: python3 -m unittest tests.test_dockerfile_run_changed_paths
heavy_cases.checks: Static chmod RUN changed-path manifests include wildcard-expanded rootfs paths for /usr/local/bin/pdocker-* and fall back to full snapshots for unsafe, relative, env-expanded, or unmatched patterns.

heavy_cases.command: Build a Dockerfile whose RUN creates, chmods, renames, links, symlinks, and deletes files while PDOCKER_DIRECT_TRACE_PATHS=1 records a changed-path manifest artifact consumed by snapshot_layer.
heavy_cases.checks: The post-RUN snapshot reports mode=paths, includes changed paths and whiteouts from traced mutations, avoids a broad rootfs walk, and preserves Dockerfile semantics for unmatched globs or shell-expanded commands by falling back to scan mode.

known_gaps.reason: No executable device artifact yet for attach-before-start, PTY resize, Ctrl-C, EOF drain, and signal-coded wait status.

Current linkat emulation copies regular file bytes, so hardlink paths do not share st_dev/st_ino, st_nlink stays 1, later writes can diverge, and crash/restart recovery for any future hardlink index or CoW metadata is not evidenced.
Implementation now uses userland readlinkat emulation without rootfs helper symlinks; Android device evidence for self/thread-self/pid exe variants is still pending.
Fast chmod wildcard manifests exist, but general RUN mutation manifests from direct syscall tracing are not implemented.

phase2_contracts.close_requires: Device artifact covering attach-before-start, exec -it, PTY resize, Ctrl-C/SIGINT, SIGTERM, EOF drain, and Docker-compatible wait/inspect status. The status assertion must explicitly cover 128+signal for signaled roots.

A syscall-specific parity matrix for every emulated success/errno path with Linux-compatible errno assertions, not generic allow/deny checks. The parity matrix must include ENOSYS fallbacks and openat2 probes.

todo_markers: harden bind, project volume, and named volume path rewrite
phase2_contracts.close_requires: One device scenario proving filesystem syscalls and AF_UNIX socket paths use identical mediation for bind, project volume, named volume, and rootfs paths.

Non-promoting until an Android device artifact proves true linkat hardlink semantics versus the current copy fallback: both names must report the same st_dev/st_ino, st_nlink must increase after linkat and decrement after unlink, writes through either name must be visible through the other, invalid flags and mediated escapes must return Linux-compatible errno, and restart recovery evidence must prove partial hardlink/CoW metadata updates fail closed without promoting divergent copies.

Device evidence proves readlinkat(/proc/self/exe), /proc/thread-self/exe, and /proc/<pid>/exe return the container guest executable path without creating /tmp/.pdocker-proc-exe-* or other rootfs helper symlinks. Required close evidence remains Android device evidence for all proc exe variants.
Direct executor emits a traced filesystem mutation manifest for RUN create/metadata/rename/link/delete operations and snapshot_layer consumes it without broad rootfs walking.
